Knighted by Thomas Radclyffe, Earl of Sussex, Lord President of the North
Lent Queen Elizabeth on her privy seal
Member of Parliament for Northumberland, although he lived in County Durham
Sir 'Thom. Hylton', probably Sir William Hilton,
One of the commissioner of the borders surveying
Rated at £200 for lands around Chester (Le Street) 'and for apparel two horsemen,
The gaol was full of criminals, including murderers, who worked together. Lord Hunsdon
After the defeat of the Spanish Armada Hilton received a special mention in Queen Elizabeth’s

zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William Hilton於1540年之前出生在英國達勒姆時，他的父親William Hilton Esquire23歲，母親Margaret Metcalfe19歲。他於1557年與Anne Yorke結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他於1576年在英國達勒姆被列為sheriff of durham。他埋葬在英國達勒姆希爾頓。

nameMeanings.name: Hilton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational name from any of various places called Hilton (e.g. in Cambridgeshire, Derbyshire, Staffordshire, North Yorkshire, and Westmorland), most of which derive from Old English hyll ‘hill’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’, though some come from Old English helde ‘slope’ + tūn.

occasionally, as in the case of Ralph filius Hilton (Yorkshire 1219), perhaps from the personal name Hilton, itself a possible form of Norman Hildun, composed of the ancient Germanic elements hild ‘strife, battle’ + hūn ‘bear cub’. Compare Hilt . This English surname is present in Ireland (mainly taken to Ulster in the early 17th century, though recorded earlier in Dublin).

variants.name: Hulton
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from Hulton (Lancashire) or Hulton (Staffordshire), both from Old English hyll ‘hill’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Compare Hilton .

variants.name: Hilt
meanings: German: from a short form of Hildebrand or some other compound ancient Germanic name formed with hild ‘strife, battle’ as the first element. See Hild .
English (London): from the Middle English personal name Hilte, ultimately from ancient Germanic Helto, probably from halith- ‘hero’.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William Hilton was born before 1540, in Durham, England, his father, William Hilton Esquire, was 23 and his mother, Margaret Metcalfe, was 19. He married Anne Yorke in 1557. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. In 1576, his occupation is listed as sheriff of durham in Durham, England. He was buried in Hilton, Durham, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir William Hilton est né avant 1540, à Durham, Angleterre, son père, William Hilton Esquire, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Margaret Metcalfe, avait 19 ans. Il a épousé Anne Yorke en 1557. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. En 1576, sa profession de son indiquée est sheriff of durham à Durham, Angleterre. Ilenterré à Hilton, Durham, Angleterre.

Lui sposato Anne Yorke nel 1557. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. Nel 1576, la sua occupazione è indicata come sheriff of durham nel Durham, England. Fu sepolto a Hilton, Durham, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir William Hiltonが1540年以前にイギリスダルハムで生まれた時，父William Hilton Esquireは23歳，母Margaret Metcalfeは19歳であった。1557年にAnne Yorkeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。1576年，イギリスダルハムでの職業はsheriff of durhamと記録されている。彼はイギリスダルハムヒルトンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William Hilton(이)가 1540년 이전 영국 뒤람에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Hilton Esquire(은)는 23세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Metcalfe(은)는 19세였다. 그는 1557년 Anne Yorke(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 1576년, 그의 직업은 영국 뒤람에서 sheriff of durham이었다. 그는 영국 뒤람 힐턴에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William Hilton родился перед 1540 г. в местности дурам, Англия, его отцу по имени William Hilton Esquire было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Margaret Metcalfe, 19. Он женился на женщине по имени Anne Yorke в 1557 г. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. В 1576 году он работал по профессии sheriff of durham в местности дурам, Англия. Он был похоронен в местности хилтон, дурам, Англия.
